ESI_SCOPES = [
    'publicData',
    'esi-contracts.read_character_contracts.v1',
    'esi-location.read_location.v1',
    'esi-location.read_ship_type.v1',

issue_title: augment_keypoints not actually deterministic when using to_deterministic()
issue_body:
In the below code - I start with three identical sets of KeypointsOnImage(), but applying a deterministic transformation leads to different results for different KeypointsOnImage() sets.

code block:

```
import imgaug as ia
from imgaug import augmenters as iaa

kps = [[ia.Keypoint(x=31, y=31),
        ia.Keypoint(x=563, y=31),
        ia.Keypoint(x=31, y=477),
        ia.Keypoint(x=563, y=477)],
       [ia.Keypoint(x=31, y=31),
        ia.Keypoint(x=563, y=31),
        ia.Keypoint(x=31, y=477),
        ia.Keypoint(x=563, y=477)],
       [ia.Keypoint(x=31, y=31),
        ia.Keypoint(x=563, y=31),
        ia.Keypoint(x=31, y=477),
        ia.Keypoint(x=563, y=477)]]

keypoint_sets = [ia.KeypointsOnImage(kp, shape=(600, 492, 3)) for kp in kps]

seq = iaa.Sequential(
    [
        iaa.Fliplr(0.5),
        iaa.Flipud(0.5),
    ]
)

seq_det = seq.to_deterministic()
keypoint_sets_aug = seq_det.augment_keypoints(keypoint_sets)

print("keypoint_sets: \n{}".format(keypoint_sets))
print("keypoint_sets_aug: \n{}".format(keypoint_sets_aug))
```


shell output (from running the above):
```
> python transmorgify.py 
keypoint_sets: 
[
  KeypointsOnImage([Keypoint(x=31, y=31), Keypoint(x=563, y=31), Keypoint(x=31, y=477), Keypoint(x=563, y=477)], shape=(600, 492, 3)),
  KeypointsOnImage([Keypoint(x=31, y=31), Keypoint(x=563, y=31), Keypoint(x=31, y=477), Keypoint(x=563, y=477)], shape=(600, 492, 3)),
  KeypointsOnImage([Keypoint(x=31, y=31), Keypoint(x=563, y=31), Keypoint(x=31, y=477), Keypoint(x=563, y=477)], shape=(600, 492, 3))
]

keypoint_sets_aug: 
[
  KeypointsOnImage([Keypoint(x=460, y=31), Keypoint(x=-72, y=31), Keypoint(x=460, y=477), Keypoint(x=-72, y=477)], shape=(600, 492, 3)),
  KeypointsOnImage([Keypoint(x=31, y=568), Keypoint(x=563, y=568), Keypoint(x=31, y=122), Keypoint(x=563, y=122)], shape=(600, 492, 3)),
  KeypointsOnImage([Keypoint(x=460, y=31), Keypoint(x=-72, y=31), Keypoint(x=460, y=477), Keypoint(x=-72, y=477)], shape=(600, 492, 3))
]
```

(the second KeypointsOnImage set is different in this case)

this does consistently reproduce for me

Please let me know if I'm miss-understanding the intended behavior of to_deterministic, but I believe that in this case, the expected behavior is for identical Keypoints to be transformed identically (even if they are in different groupings of KeypointsOnImage)

issue_title: SIUnitx Formatter can't handle fractional powers
issue_body:
Hello,
I am using Pint 0.6, but I also set up a separate function to duplicate the siunitx formatter for myself, since it doesn't exist in 0.6. In working with it, I found that it is unable to print fractional powers (e.g. 0.5) because the power is expected to be an integer: https://github.com/hgrecco/pint/blob/5c409ea6695170b74f63ba39fbc64162c482730b/pint/formatting.py#L206

A simple fix is to test if the power is an integer:
    
    return r'\tothe{{{:d}}}'.format(int(power)) if power.is_integer() else r'\tothe{{{:.1f}}}'.format(power)

Of course, knowing the precision required for the float is the next problem.

issue_title: Add examples for diff conflicting files
issue_body:
Diffting conflict files is as easy as doing:

    $ cdiff :1:file :2:file
    $ cdiff :1:file :3:file

Where:

- 1: is base
- 2: is ours
- 3: is theirs

Things get complicated easily when using rebase command, where our changes become theirs and viceversa

Will it be helpful to add options like

    $ cdiff --conflict-12 file
    $ cdiff --conflict-13 file
    $ cdiff --conflict-23 file

?

Maybe a `--reverse` option can be added to offer `--conflict-21`, etc. Or `--rebase-12` that implies `--conflict-12 --reverse` etc

issue_title: s3transfer not excluded in zip file, causing compatibility issues
issue_body:
Kappa excludes folders like boto3 by default in its packaging process. However, s3transfer is installed during the installation of boto3:

> from https://github.com/boto/boto3/blob/1.4.4/requirements.txt
> -e git://github.com/boto/botocore.git@develop#egg=botocore
> -e git://github.com/boto/jmespath.git@develop#egg=jmespath
> **-e git://github.com/boto/s3transfer.git@develop#egg=s3transfer**
> nose==1.3.3
> mock==1.3.0
> wheel==0.24.0
> unittest2==0.5.1; python_version == '2.6'

The folder, s3transfer is not excluded during the kappa installation process. 

This causes compatibility issues when user has a boto3 version different from the current Lambda buildin, and uploads a function with kappa.

To reproduce:
- put a version A of boto3 that is different from the current AWS Lambda buildin version B
- use kappa to deploy a function
- now deployed function uses the s3transfer library from version A and other parts of boto3 from version B

issue_title: IAPWS97 : Computation of Gamma
issue_body:
Hi Juan José,

I think there is a problem in the computation of gamma in your IAPWS97 library. I notice a factor 1000 between your IAPWS97 and IAPWS95 gamma values.
`fase.gamma = -self.v/self.P/1000*self.derivative("P", "v", "s", fase)`
should be :
`fase.gamma = -self.v/self.P*self.derivative("P", "v", "s", fase)`
You can also compute gamma as well if you do not want to use derivatives:
`fase.gamma = 1.e-6*self.w**2/self.P/self.v`           (if P is in MPa)
I use this last expression in my C code and the results are close to RefProp and IAPWS95 results.
Regards.

`from thermo import chemical

issue_title: an idea about subparams
issue_body:
Right now, say you had a controller that could have a path:

```python
class Foo(Controller):
    def GET(self, action, **kwargs): pass
```

where `action` can be *bar* or *che*, so valid requests would be:

* /example.com/foo/bar
* /example.com/foo/che

I'm wondering if we could codify this with camel casing:

```python
class FooBar(Controller):
    """handle /foo/bar"""
    def GET(self, **kwargs): pas

class FooChe(Controller):
    """handle /foo/che"""
    def GET(self, **kwargs): pas
```

So camel casing class names would move through path bits. This is inspired by some comments Jarid made about us having to set a few params as optional because we had multiple action path bits that needed different attributes:

```python
class Foo(Controller):
    @param(0, choices=["one", "two"], help="the action value")
    @param("bar", default=None, help="only needed if action is 'one' but needs to be optional")
    @param("che", default=None, help="only needed if action is 'two' but needs to be optional")
    def GET(self, action, **kwargs): pass
```

issue_title: Last astropy version doesn't build in python2
issue_body:
When building I bumped into an issue I had for another tool before: the current development version of astropy doesn't build with python 2 but is still the default being downloaded if you don't specify anything in the requirements.txt when using python setup.py. 

A simple fix, for now at least, is to replace "astropy" by "astropy<3.0" as in that file and it builds like a charm. When using pip, either in the way you use in in travis, or as in the documentation works well. I woud actually advice you to make sure that the installation in travis and the installation instructions in the README are the same.

issue_title: Possible to get list of postseason games?
issue_body:
If I'm understanding the API documentation, I should be able to do this to get a list of games for the first week of this year's playoffs:

    games = nflgame.games(2014, week=1, kind='POST')

However, when I try to do this, Python errors out with:
```
  File "/sbbs/xtrn/sportsstats/nfl.py", line 131, in parseSchedule
    games = nflgame.games(year, week=theweek, kind=season)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/nflgame/__init__.py", line 219, in games
    return list(games_gen(year, week, home, away, kind, started))
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
```

This code works just fine for regular season games. I'm only getting errors when I switch to postseason. Am I doing something wrong, or is this a problem on nflgame's side?

issue_title: pager_ajaxProcessing could be a useful default function
issue_body:
See [widgets/widget-pager.js#L94](https://github.com/Mottie/tablesorter/blob/master/js/widgets/widget-pager.js#L94) - if you don't configure it, it returns nothing, even if your ajax endpoint already returns the data in exactly the format you would need.

In that case you then have to everywhere configure `pager_ajaxProcessing: function(data){return data}`.

Could that not be the default?

Truncation of stdout
issue_body:
I have a command that sends 600kB of output to stdout.
```
cat /path/to/file.fastq | seqtk sample - 3656 | seqtk seq -a -
```

Called with `os.system()`, it does exactly that, however, when run through Envoy the output is truncated to 6kB. I've tried using the inbuilt `.std_out`, redirection, and `tee`ing to an output file, but the output is always truncated. Is this a problem related to pipes? I have many calls to `envoy.run()` in the same script – does a buffer need flushing? I'm unfamiliar with the finer points of subprocess – is this a problem inherited from subprocess, or is it envoy, or something else entirely?

issue_title: WRAP has no effect on LASFile.write method
issue_body:
The files being written out are always unwrapped, with WRAP remaining however it was in the original LASFile object.

The correct behaviour should follow the specs 

- 1.2 spec: 
  - WRAP is NO, max data line including all chars is 256 (in practice I think v1.2 files output from lasio with WRAP NO should have unlimited data line length but have a logger.warning message)
  - WRAP is YES, max data line including all chars is 80
- 2.0 spec:
  - WRAP is NO, max data line length unlimited
  - WRAP is YES max data line length 80 chars

Note that the spec says nothing about header line length. I think given modern software that we should allow unlimited header line length but provide a logger.warning message if the file has WRAP YES and any of the header lines are > 80 chars.

issue_title: Recent release creates very hard to debug errors
issue_body:
This commit https://github.com/lixxu/sanic-jinja2/commit/cd1cb9747cce1ed66a25d8582fd73a25c52a3929 is incomplete. It is incomplete because when an app is calling `init_app` later in their code, it will not make aware of the default `app_name` argument being set to `None`.

Therefore it will pass `None` to the Jinja2 Loader which then throws a cryptic `ImportError`:

```
File "/media/ephemeral0/ben/app.aca9088a3a8cc36fc7bddd72ee32e56c12edc24c/app/__init__.py", line 83, in make_app
  jinja2_env.init_app(app)
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/sanic_jinja2/__init__.py", line 31, in init_app
  loader = PackageLoader(pkg_name, 'templates')
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/jinja2/loaders.py", line 224, in __init__
  provider = get_provider(package_name)
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 435, in get_provider
  __import__(moduleOrReq)
TypeError: __import__() argument 1 must be str, not None
```

How is anyone going to debug this? 

Suggested remediation:

- Add an exception for this case (`if app_name is None: raise ValueError('need an app name!')`)

and/or

- `if app_name is None: app_name = app.name`

issue_title: ApplicationSettings.debug option causing GUI stuttering when calling Python functions from JS
issue_body:
According to https://groups.google.com/d/msg/cefpython/NJUDIm81D3A/eBvHwJprCgAJ
it is the "debug" option that was causing the delays.

CEF Python writes to "debug.log" file using its own logger, however at the same time CEF is also writing to that same file. Maybe there is some conflict, something to do with lock acquired on the file, I don't know. Maybe CEF Python should use CEF log mechanism and that would fix it. It sounds like this is the cause. Here is the code in DebugLog.h:

```
FILE* pFile = fopen(g_logFile.c_str(), "a");
fprintf(pFile, "[CEF Python] App: %s\n", szString);
fclose(pFile);
```

It opens and closes the file for each single log write. It might be that CEF that also writes to this file,
opens it during cef initialization, acquires some lock and closes it at shutdown.


----------

## OLD info

**UPDATE**: 
- It seems the issue occurs only on Windows 10, see [this post](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/cefpython/NJUDIm81D3A/PgwmHJMBFwAJ)
- Testing by another user shows that the py<>js and js<>py communication times are about 15 ms, so there isn't anything unusual in it - see [this post](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/cefpython/mwSa7He90xA/0FCbILs-DQAJ)
- The bug doesn't reproduce on Linux in v54 nor v31. Added time measure in unit tests in commit 6ffb865c0a95346e9343832ec4405f3591ce7ab8. So it seems to be a Windows only issue.

When calling Python function from Javascript there is a stuttering of CSS animation during the time of the call. It looks like the UI thread is being locked for a moment. Reported on the Forum: https://groups.google.com/d/topic/cefpython/NJUDIm81D3A/discussion

At the end of this post there is presented a temporary solution that could be used for now, and two possible real solutions.

This looks like a GIL issue. GIL is a global interpreter lock in Python. Information found on the web:
- Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_interpreter_lock
- Understanding the Python GIL presentation: http://www.dabeaz.com/python/UnderstandingGIL.pdf
- Python wiki: https://wiki.python.org/moin/GlobalInterpreterLock
- From [Cython documentation](http://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/userguide/external_C_code.html#acquiring-and-releasing-the-gil):
  > Note that acquiring the GIL is a blocking thread-synchronising operation, and therefore potentially costly. It might not be worth releasing the GIL for minor calculations. Usually, I/O operations and substantial computations in parallel code will benefit from it.

Looks like the issue in question is only a momentary lock of the UI thread while acquiring GIL. Python <> Javascript calls are asynchronous, use IPC messaging between processes. Javascript runs in the renderer process, while Python runs in the browser process. You can execute time intensive tasks in Python using multiprocessing or similar, while not blocking the UI.

I recall a similar issue of UI locking when [OnBeforePopup is being called in wxpython.py](https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/d199672a9cd7590903a058e09bab519c3d0c9141/src/windows/binaries_32bit/wxpython.py#L640), in which a task is being posted on the UI thread to create a popup window.

Related information:
- Issue #102 "Remove GIL to avoid deadlocks when calling CEF functions"
- In [MessageLoop and MessageLoopWork](https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/15a206343489207eb75d86906c5bdd64e71c8b23/src/cefpython.pyx#L824) functions GIL is being released when calling to CEF's functions. In a comment to MessageLoopWork there is a comment stating that GIL must be released otherwise you get a dead lock when calling from c++ to python.

This are the steps during communication from Javascript to Python:
1. Javascript runs in the renderer process. A C++ handler to javascript binded function is being called. An asynchronous IPC message is being sent to the browser process, using Chromium's inter-process communication.
2. In the browser process a CEF's C++ callback [OnProcessMessageReceived](https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/15a206343489207eb75d86906c5bdd64e71c8b23/src/client_handler/client_handler.cpp#L86) is being called. From this callback we are calling into Python using [ExecutePythonCallback](https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/15a206343489207eb75d86906c5bdd64e71c8b23/src/python_callback.pyx#L68) Cython function. This func was declared "with gil" which means that GIL lock is acquired automatically.

Things to consider:
1. Is it really a GIL issue? Confirm. Print times in OnProcessMessageReceived and in ExecutePythonCallback to see how much time it took to acquire the GIL.
2. Maybe it's a CEF/Chromium threading issue that makes acquiring GIL so much time consuming?
3. Maybe that's just some Cython issue when acquiring GIL during a call from C/C++ code? I have hard time believing that acquiring GIL takes so much time, as presented in the video by the original reporter.
4. How does wxPython and alike frameworks handle GIL issue? Are there some workarounds?
5. The issue was reproduced on Windows with CEF Python 31. Does it still behave the same in latest CEF Python 54+? (Linux releases currently only available)
6. (+) Does the issue occur also on other platforms with such long time for the UI thread being locked? (Mac, Linux)
   - (WORKS OK) Linux checked and the issue doesn't reproduce with latest CEF Python v54. Added a unit test to measure time of executing Python function and then back js callback that was passed. Time elapsed = 11 ms. Everything works immediately with no locks. Commit 6ffb865c0a95346e9343832ec4405f3591ce7ab8.
7. Does it behave any differently in Python 3?

## Temporary solution

There is an alternative for js<>py communication, from README.md:

> For native communication between javascript and python use javascript bindings. Another option is to > run an internal python web server and use websockets/XMLHttpRequest for js<>python communication.

Using web server and XHR for communication wouldn't require any acquiring/releasing GIL, thus the issue should disappear I think. 

## Solution 1 (multi threaded message loop)

**UPDATE**: This improves UI locking when calling js callbacks from Python, but there is still some GUI locking when calling Python func from javascript. It seems that the final solution would need to be to use multi threaded message loop along with implementing Python-native IPC messaging with the renderer processes, without the Chromium/CEF IPC layer so that GIL doesn't need to be acquired when receiving messages (Solution 2).

If it turns out this is a Windows-only problem, then I think that there is an alternative solution, to use ApplicationSettings.multi_threaded_message_loop (Issue #133). Message loop will run in a separate thread and any calls to Python won't block UI. However whether this would also resolve issue with GIL acquiring lock taking much time, I don't know, but for sure it won't lock UI (css animation). I haven't yet played with multi threaded message loop, so don't know how this would affect current code base. There may be some issues that would need to be resolved.

## Solution 2 (Python-native IPC messaging with the renderer process)

I think that it should be doable to avoid using CEF/Chromium inter-process messaging. If the issue is the GIL and we can get rid of the GIL being acquired when calling from CEF C++ function into Python in the browser process, then it should solve the UI lock issue.

We could handle IPC messaging in the browser process, directly with Python. In the renderer process we would need some cross-platform C++ code. Googled ["python ipc c++"](https://www.google.com/search?q=python+ipc+c%2B%2B&oq=python+ipc+c%2B%2B&sourceid=chrome) and found [zeromq](http://zeromq.org/) library that allows for sending IPC messages and can connect with any language.

issue_title: VectorBus: permissionMask should be set before calling xlOpenPort
issue_body:
@ Version 2.0.0b1

The permissionMask parameter is both input & output to xlOpenPort and must be set before the call to xlOpenPort in order to request "init access" permission.  This is necessary, for example, if the bitrate is to be changed later with a call to xlCanSetChannelBitrate.

I.e. In class VectorBus...

  ```
   permission_mask = vxlapi.XLaccess()
   # HERE: Set mask to request init permission if needed
   if bitrate:
       permission_mask.value = self.mask   
   vxlapi.xlOpenPort(self.port_handle, self._app_name, self.mask,
                          permission_mask, rx_queue_size, ...
```
Failure to set this mask results in the following warning when trying to set the bitrate.

`'Can not set bitrate since no init access' `

issue_title: Suggestion: HAP-python-min
issue_body:
I was thinking if it would make sense to add a new **pypi** project: `HAP-python-min`. This would include all content from `pyhap` except for the `accessories` folder.

I'm currently working on a component for [Home Assistant](https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant). Some of the examples form the accessories folder may work, but to port of all functions is basically more difficult than to write a new class which extends `accessory`. Because the initial code base for Home Assistant is already big their is a focus to keep additional imports as small as possible to improve performance. A `min` version would help accomplish that.

def runstrat(args=None):

            print(positions)
            print('-- TRANSACTIONS')
            print(transactions)
            print('-- GROSS LEVERAGE')
            print(gross_lev)

        import pyfolio as pf
        pf.create_full_tear_sheet(
            returns,
            positions=positions,
            transactions=transactions,
            gross_lev=gross_lev,
            live_start_date='2012-09-01',
            round_trips=True)

    if args.plot:
        cerebro.plot(style=args.plot_style)
def parse_args(args=None):

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
        description='Sample for pivot point and cross plotting')

    parser.add_argument('--fromdate', required=False,
                        default='2012-09-01',
                        help='Starting date in YYYY-MM-DD format')

    parser.add_argument('--todate', required=False,
                        default='2016-12-31',

                        help=('Cash to start with'))

    parser.add_argument('--plot', required=False, action='store_true',
                        help=('Plot the result'))

    parser.add_argument('--plot-style', required=False, action='store',
                        default='bar', choices=['bar', 'candle', 'line'],
                        help=('Plot style'))

    parser.add_argument('--no-pyfolio', required=False, action='store_true',
                        help=('Do not do pyfolio things'))

    aargs = args if args is not None else sys.argv[1:]
    return parser.parse_args(aargs)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    runstrat()

issue_title: Simple Error Model
issue_body:
Issue to track the progress on the [Roadmap](https://github.com/HadrienG/InSilicoSeq/issues/1) item "Add a simple error model"

I guess that the simplest would be to:

- [x] Add 2 parameters: mean_quality and std_dev*
- [x] Generate random quality scores following a normal distribution
- [x] Eventually modify the nucleotides whose quality aren't perfect (it never is)
- [x] write docstrings and tests

A few unknowns:
- Should the length of the sequences vary?
- To which base switch if we get an erroneous call. A random other nucleotide? I would guess a random nucl. is good enough for a simple error model.

\* I haven't added a standard deviation parameter. it is hardcoded to 0.01 but can be discussed

issue_title: Dont completely remove variable galaxy when overirde in constant
issue_body:
I setup a phase with two galaxies:
```
    phase1 = LensProfileGalaxy1Phase(lens_galaxies=[gp.GalaxyPrior(elliptical_sersic=lp.EllipticalSersicLP),
                                                                                        gp.GalaxyPrior(elliptical_sersic=lp.EllipticalSersicLP)])
```

I then over ride the first galaxy with constants in pass priors

```
    class LensProfileGalaxy1Phase(ph.LensProfilePhase):
        def pass_priors(self, previous_results):
            self.lens_galaxies[0] = previous_results[0].constant.lens_galaxies[0]
```

This means that the results from the analysis now only has one galaxy in lens_galaxies.variable, and two in lens_galaxies.constant. Furthermore, it means that what we call result.lens_galaxies[0].variable corresponds to result.lens_galaxies[1].constant.

Is it possible for the lens galaxy overirde to not completely remove the variable lens galaxy and keep the list structure in order? I can work around for now but its unintuitive.

issue_title: ConfigManager.export improvements
issue_body:
Add kwarg to ConfigManager.export to change keys from sub-names to sub-paths

`ConfigManager.export()` now returns `[('uploads.threads', 5), ('uploads.enabled', 'yes')]`, but knowing that path segments can contain dots this is not reversible. Perhaps by default should return:

`[(('uploads', 'threads'), 5), (('uploads', 'enabled'), 'yes')]`

and only return the current primitive format with a certain `kwarg` i.e. `key='name'`, default being `key='path'`

Also, it isn't anywhere specified whether `.export` returns string values or typed values.

issue_title: what do you mean by this "Please pull from master on repository and run install library, " regarding get history
prewhiten_magseries(), possible conflicting optional args
issue_body:
When running prewhiten_magseries() in lcfit.py, if the argument fourierorder is specified but fourierparam is left alone, then a warning triggers at line 331 and fourierparam is used to the exclusion of fourierorder (see link below)

https://github.com/waqasbhatti/astrobase/blob/master/astrobase/varbase/lcfit.py#L331

fourierorder can be used to the exclusion of fourierparam if fourierparam is set to, e.g., an empty list.  However, it seems a little problematic that setting one optional argument without resetting another one from its default value makes the freshly set optional argument unused.  It took me a little bit to notice this behavior, even though a warning does get displayed when both arguments are set.  Given the current defaults (fourierparam set, fourierorder not set), it may make more sense for the behavior when both get set to be that fourierorder is given preference over fourierparam.  Or maybe the default values of both should be unset, and then the code, if it detects both are still unset, then assigned fourierparam to have its current default value.

issue_title: On naming
issue_body:
As I’ve already mentioned by e-mail, I’m *strongly* opposed to call this concept “data classes”.

Having an easy way to define many small class with attributes is nothing about data, it’s about good OO design.

Calling it “data classes” implies that they differ from…“code classes” I guess?

One of the things people love about attrs is that it’s helping them to write regular classes which they can add methods to without any subclassing or other magic.  IOW: to focus on the **actual code** they want to write as opposed to generic boilerplate.

Debasing them by name seems like a poor start to me.  We *do* have data containers in the stdlib (namedtuples, SimpleNamespace) so I don’t see a reason to add a third to the family – even if just by name.

issue_title: unhandled RequestTimeout exceptions
issue_body:
https://github.com/ashleysommer/sanic-cors/blob/master/sanic_cors/extension.py#L177

When a 'keep-alive' connection reaches timeout, Sanic raises an RequestTimeout exception. This case the `wrapped_function` gets `None` for `req`. The result is an `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'url'`. Moreover the original handler doesn't get a chance for proper handling because of the raised `AttributeError`

issue_title: Emphasis spanning sections
issue_body:
Came across an issue when parsing https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User_talk:JoeHebda&oldid=718781560

There's an unclosed emphasis in a section that causes the nodes list to contain a single item that spans several sections up until the next emphasis. I've attached the smallest amount of wikitext that I could extract that exemplifies the behaviour. Here's example code that'll show the problem:

```python
import mwparserfromhell, codecs
with codecs.open('mwparserfromhell_testcase.txt', 'r', 'utf-8') as infile:
     wikitext = ''.join(infile)
ptext = mwparserfromhell.parse(wikitext)
```

Now, `ptext.nodes[6]` should contain a large block of text. The problem is technically found in `ptext.nodes[2]`, where the emphasis is not closed properly, causing mwparserfromhell to match the emphasis parts in (perhaps) unexpected ways.

[mwparserfromhell_testcase.txt](https://github.com/earwig/mwparserfromhell/files/251280/mwparserfromhell_testcase.txt)

issue_title: Fix travis OSX build
issue_body: Travis has enabled the OSX support for this repository, but that is not (yet) compatible with the python mode we have been using. We need to wait for travis to fix this, or set up our own Python environment in the case of OSX.

Can you please add the status code to the exception?

issue_title: overflow when calculating nChroma in stChromaFeaturesInit(nfft, fs)
issue_body:
When fs = 8000,
nfft=80,
the flowing code in  stChromaFeaturesInit(nfft, fs) will  yield a 'nChroma' that contains values > nfft.

freqs = numpy.array([((f + 1) * fs) / (2 * nfft) for f in range(nfft)])
Cp = 27.50

    from flask import Markup

issue_title: Transaction compiler
issue_body:
many thanks for this work. I moved to something else, otherwise would have liked to contributed more. some things can be seen here, but I couldn't merge them anywhere, and abandoned  the effort: https://github.com/LykkeCity/Notary/tree/master/glue, also because upstream moved to NBitcoin exclusively.

The idea was to have something like a transaction compiler or builder to provide an easy to use interface for user-space. The problem is that bitcoin-core requires tremendous amount of expertise to compile transactions. Currently advanced transactions are used only by handful of experts in the world. python-bitcoinlib could be an intermediary layer, but its not quite that (not its purpose probably in any case).  Referencing previous inputs alone is not trivial. To move some money one needs to know "where" that money is stored. To arrive at some code which in 3 lines of code moves money from A to B, seems to be not really possible and the way I see it now requires redesign of the entire stack as it relates to UTXO way of accounting itself. NBitcoin to some extent allows to do that with the transaction builder.

Hi, sorry if it may already asked but I sincerely can't figure out what's wrong since I believe having followed all installation steps of zinnia-wysiwyg-tinymce.
I get a no reverse for tinymce-js when I try to edit an entry in the admin panel.
Is there something related to the admin template?

issue_title: Style tags created on render are discarted
issue_body:
Style tags created with **[`Renderer.insert_style_in_content`](https://github.com/christopher-ramirez/secretary/blob/24e4cb0ad3b8d72d4c8627d0464ee31b14da31d6/secretary.py#L581)** method while rendering a template are not included in the final document.

This is happening because `insert_style_in_content` is working over `self.content` which is then overwritten on line [487](https://github.com/christopher-ramirez/secretary/blob/24e4cb0ad3b8d72d4c8627d0464ee31b14da31d6/secretary.py#L487).

issue_title: 0.9.1 fix doesn't call disconnect handler when a client timeout
issue_body:
Hi Miguel,

Thanks for this 0.9.1 fix, I believe this is for the issue I posted related to the thread handling redis dying when there was an error thrown.  After this fix has been added. It seems to be working well now but currently i'm encountering a different error.  I reduced the ping_interval = 2, or a very low number. issue_title: FragilityFunctionDiscrete can not handle list properly
issue_body:
Noticed that FragilityFunctionDiscrete can not handle list or array properly as below:
`a =  FragilityFunctionDiscrete(limit_state='slight', 
                                                     `imls` = [0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1, 1.2, 1.4],
                                                      poes = [0.0, 0.771, 0.95, 0.989, 0.997, 0.999, 1., 1.],
                                                     no_damage_limit=0.05)
`
It works okay when it takes a single value, but it fails when it takes array and yields a wrong value if it takes a list:

`a(0.4)` 
`a([0.2, 0.4])`
`a(numpy.array([0.2, 0.4]))`

I changed the code as below to fix the error:
`class FragilityFunctionDiscrete(object):

    def __call__(self, iml):
        """
        Compute the Probability of Exceedance (PoE) for the given
        Intensity Measure Level (IML).
        """
        highest_iml = self.imls[-1]

        # when the intensity measure level is above
        # the range, we use the highest one
        try:
            iml = [highest_iml if x > highest_iml else x for x in iml]
        except TypeError:
            temp = self.interp(highest_iml if iml > highest_iml else iml)
        else:
            temp = self.interp(iml)

        if self.no_damage_limit:
             temp[iml < self.no_damage_limit] = 0.0
        return temp
`

Hi all, 

`CloudSearchDomain.Client`'s `search()` method takes a keyword argument named `return`, which can cause an unexpected `SyntaxError`. Unpacking a dict of keyword arguments is a perfectly fine workaround, but it would be nice if it were documented here: https://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/cloudsearchdomain.html#CloudSearchDomain.Client.search. (The "Request Syntax" section of that documentation also shows an example that throws a `SyntaxError`). Here's a quick console example: 

```ipython
In [2]: from search_service import cloudsearch_domain

In [3]: cloudsearch_domain
Out[3]: <botocore.client.CloudSearchDomain at 0x7fbe04726588>

In [4]: cloudsearch_domain.search(query='my great query')
Out[4]:
{'ResponseMetadata': {'HTTPStatusCode': 200},
 'hits': {'found': 0, 'hit': [], 'start': 0},
 'status': {'rid': 'tunPuJkqtgkKjJEj', 'timems': 42}}

                                                           ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax

Out[6]:

issue_title: Option to disable -Ofast
issue_body:
Hello,

I am currently working on VAEs & parsing [1].
One requirement in this setting is to compute the entropy of the distribution over parse trees (i.e. for the KL divergence w.r.t. a flat prior).
To prevent computational errors (underflow, overflow, etc), we have to:
- store signed values in log domain, see [2]
- rely on careful implementation of operations like log(1+exp(x)), see [3]

However, the makefile of dynet forces the -Ofast option, which in turn force the -ffast-math option.
Therefore, we still encounter computational errors (e.g. exp(-92) results in an error).

To bypass this problem, I manually change the configuration and replace -Ofast with -O3 here:
https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt#L85

Would you accept a pull request that:
- either replace -Ofast with -O3? (I ***think*** that Eigen use build in math operations, so it should not impact Dynet)
- or add an option that controls CMake so the user can manually change it, e.g. -DGCC_O=3, -DGCC_O=fast, -DGCC_O=2, etc...

[1] https://arxiv.org/abs/1807.09875
[2] http://www.cs.jhu.edu/~zfli/pubs/semiring_translation_zhifei_emnlp09.pdf
[3] https://cran.r-project.org/package=Rmpfr/vignettes/log1mexp-note.pdf

issue_title: Support special number names for numeric type in csvw.datatypes
issue_body: See https://pymotw.com/3/decimal/#special-values for how things like `Infinity` of `NaN` are represented, and http://w3c.github.io/csvw/syntax/#h-formats-for-numeric-types for what must be recognized.

issue_title: expose ports with -P by default?
Where should case studies live?
issue_body:
We put examples in the dask-examples repository, but that has hardware limitations.  What should the expectations around case studies be and where should they live?

To start, I recommend that we define these as self-contained notebooks, possibly with .py scripts or text instructions if necessary.  These might live in this repository for the time being.  These aren't as much for public consumption as they are for other developers of dask-ml to use when testing out ideas.  Ideally they would eventually be composed with dask-ml capabilities to create a few blogposts each.

Eventually it would be useful to test them on small datasets frequently and benchmark them with larger datasets infrequently, but this sort of infrastructure can come later (though it's good to have in mind)

issue_title: Docs reorganization
issue_body:
I've been walking through the documentation and had a few notes.  I'd be happy to make these changes but wanted to check in before I submitted anything.  

The separation between single machine and distributed learning seems odd to me.  Many of the topics listed in single machine (grid search, pipelining, possibly even incremental learning) are still relevant when on a cluster.  

I might instead flatten the TOC to just remove the single-machine/distributed distinction, and give all of the subsections their own home.  This might flatten the TOC to something like the following:

1.  Pipelines
2.  Hyper-parameter search
3.  Incremental learning
4.  Generalized Linear Models
5.  Joblib
6.  XGBoost
7.  Clustering
8.  Examples
9.  API

I've also found it pleasant recently to start sections with the API relevant for that section.  [Futures docs](http://dask.pydata.org/en/latest/futures.html) for an example.  This gives a quick list at the beginning of each section on the API relevant for that section.  Those functions still link to the main API doc page.

Any thoughts or objections to this reorganization @TomAugspurger ?

issue_title: Generate "version" file with SCM info during build
issue_body:
Juju is going to be adding support for a `version` file at the root of the charm directory that should contain SCM info about exactly what is included in the charm.  `charm-build` needs to generate this file during build, and `charm-push` will grow a similar feature to add the file during push if it can.

It should support git, bzr, and hg, and should use `git describe --dirty` and other equivalent commands to include SHA and local modification info.  If `charm-build` is called on something that is not an SCM repo, it should no-op.  If there is already a `version` file in the charm source, it should be overwritten and a warning emitted indicating that it was overwritten.

issue_title: Numpy recipe broken (atlas, blas, lapack, -lcrystax)
issue_body:
I found a similar result (atlas, blas) from #1040, yet the error was different. Maybe this is what @inclement's error was. I wonder how to fix it, because I can compile Kivy, pyjnius and some other packages, yet when trying to make a pandas recipe it breaks (pandas package requires numpy).

The error is rather strange though, because iirc, the `libcrystax.so` exists on android, so we create it somewhere here (or fetch from crystax, +/- shouldn't matter in this case), so it should be able to see it, right? It doesn't make sense to me as Kivy itself surely sees the `libcrystax.so` and even the numpy's `get_libs()` is patched so that it shouldn't even be called. I checked and the packing went correctly, so... for now I have no clue about how to fix it. I'll look at the same case with using Android NDK as a replacement, though I don't think it's only about `-lcrystax` flag (found `libcrystax.so` in all crystax apps).

issue_title: Default to retrying on base Python exception
issue_body:
I'm migrating from the [retrying](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/retrying) library to `backoff` and one thing I noticed between the two libraries is that I always have to define what Exception I want to retry on when using `backoff`

Would it be viable to default to `Exception` and make defining exceptions in `on_exception` as optional?

issue_title: Missing alpha and beta values
issue_body:
I was trying to load the vgg19 model in the onnx/models repo. However, the `gemm` operator in the model doesn't seem to have to `alpha` or `beta` attributes. Also, the operator docs ([here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#Gemm)) don't mention any default values that the attributes should take in case they are missing from a  model.

Can you tell me what do these attributes default to? Also, it'd be better to add this data in the docs too.

cc @houseroad Some urgent help please.

issue_title: Missing markers for isort's futures dependency
issue_body:
isorts's [setup.py](https://github.com/timothycrosley/isort/blob/develop/setup.py) defines:

```python
install_requires=['futures; python_version < "3.2"'],
```

But the resulting `Pipfile.lock` is missing the `markers` rule for `futures`.

##### Expected result

```json
"futures": {
    "hashes": [
        "sha256:9ec02aa7d674acb8618afb127e27fde7fc68994c0437ad759fa094a574adb265",
        "sha256:ec0a6cb848cc212002b9828c3e34c675e0c9ff6741dc445cab6fdd4e1085d1f1"
    ],
    "markers": "python_version < '3.2'",
    "version": "==3.2.0"
}
```

##### Actual result

```json
"futures": {
    "hashes": [
        "sha256:9ec02aa7d674acb8618afb127e27fde7fc68994c0437ad759fa094a574adb265",
        "sha256:ec0a6cb848cc212002b9828c3e34c675e0c9ff6741dc445cab6fdd4e1085d1f1"
    ],
    "version": "==3.2.0"
}
```

##### Steps to replicate

```bash
# executed on python 2.7
$ pipenv --version
pipenv, version 9.0.3
$ pipenv install isort
$ pipenv graph
isort==4.3.4
  - futures [required: Any, installed: 3.2.0]
  - futures [required: Any, installed: 3.2.0]
$ < Pipfile.lock
```

issue_title: "update" doesn't recognize package name cased differently than in Pipfile
issue_body:
If package name case that i want to **update** differs from  the one in `Pipfile` - `pipenv` will not  see that package as installed and won't update it. On the other hand, if i want to **uninstall** that package - `pipenv` will find it and all will work fine. 

I've discovered this issue after converting my old `requirements.txt`.

##### Describe your environment

1. Windows 10, 1607
1. Python version: `Python 3.6.4`
1. Pipenv version: `pipenv, version 9.0.3`

##### Expected result

`django` should be recognized by `pipenv` as installed package while **updating**, even if in `Pipfile` package name is `Django`. This is expected behavior, since this is how it works with **uninstall** command.

##### Actual result

`django was not found in your Pipfile!` while **updating** it, but **uninstalling** works fine.

**Step 1**
Create `requirements.txt`:
`echo Django==1.11.3 > requirements.txt`

**Step 2**

`pipenv install -r requirements.txt --verbose`

issue_title: Importing pkg_resources is slow. Work is done at import time.
issue_body:
Any "import pkg_resources" by a module is slow, in the 100-150ms range, depending on the system.

This is due to the number of modules imported by pkg_resources itself (email.parser is also slow), and side-effect work done at import time, notably the `_initialize` and `_initialize_master_working_set` functions at the bottom of `pkg_resources/__init__.py`.

As wall clock time matters to humans running CLIs, every millisecond counts.

The work done at import time should be deferred until needed, and the imports themselves also deferred if possible.

issue_title: Multiple RPM packages from one setup.py
issue_body:
Hey everyone! Not sure if this is good place for this question but is it possible to build more than one RPM package from one setup.py (eg. like in SPEC file you can define more packages like `package`, `package-lib`, `package-cfg` etc.)?

Unfortunately, I haven't found anything about that, only someone mentioned about few setup.py files.

issue_title: Support requiring from developmental releases
issue_body:
With following in `setup.py`:

    install_requires=[
        'mypackage==1.0.0.dev1'
    ]

`python setup.py develop`:

```
Installed /home/musttu/XXX
Processing dependencies for wires-aws==2.2.1.dev0+ng42323d9.d20161208
Searching for mypackage==1.0.0.dev1
Reading https://INTRA-PYPI/index/+simple/mypackage/
Authenticating as musttu for https://INTRA-PYPI/index/+simple/mypackage/ (from .pypirc)
No local packages or working download links found for mypackage==1.0.0.dev1
error: Could not find suitable distribution for Requirement.parse('mypackage==1.0.0dev1')
```

Looks like it searches PyPI (or our company-devpi in this case) for matching version, but just doesn't find/accept one when you use developmental release as version identifier.

https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#developmental-releases

issue_title: [Context] Modify/set commands aren't consistent with the equivalent commands in the account group
issue_body:
The `update` verb seems to be pretty prevalent throughout the CLI, so it was unexpected to see that the `az context` command group uses `modify` instead. Additionally, `az context` uses the `switch` verb, when `az account` uses `set` for (what I can tell) a conceptually similar action. 

Is there a reason for these differences? For ergonomic/muscle memory purposes, it would be great to have as much consistency in the CLI as possible.

issue_title: Inconistent behaviour
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
 When adding a NSG rule  that has a source of AzureLoadbalancer in a inbound direct it adds successfully. When you when add an rule that has a destination of Azureloadbalancer and the direction is outbound, BUT THE SAME RULE NAME the previous rule is silently deleted.

If you attempt to do the same in the portal an error is returned and the command can not proceed.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour.

az network nsg rule create -g <ResourceGroup> --nsg-name <NSGName> -n Allow_AzureLoadbalancer --access Allow --source-address-prefixes AzureLoadBalancer --destination-port-ranges * --direction Inbound  --destination-address-prefixes <SubnetRange> --priority 900

 then run

az network nsg rule create -g <ResourceGroup> --nsg-name nsg-apppublic -n Allow_AzureLoadbalancer --access Allow --source-address-prefixes <subnet ip>  --destination-port-ranges * --direction Outbound  --destination-address-prefixes AzureLoadBalancer --priority 900

The output of the secondary command reports a success, while the first rule is removed

**Expected behavior**
I would expect the second command to fail with an error "Name in use" similar to the portal

**Environment summary**
AZ clie
Powershell
Win10 insider Ringer (Fast)

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

issue_title: Investigate how GraphQL could look like
issue_body:
* We have flat collections of records
* We have JSON schemas
* It seems very reasonnable to think that resolving a GraphQL query using our data model is feasible
* Queryable APIs are really becoming a thing, and it would make for a «reusable storage backend» to offer that
* Clients like Relay can do very powerful stuff for caching locally and syncing trees of data

Let's discuss and POC something!

* See http://graphene-python.org

issue_title: If id, schema or last_modified are marked as required, record can't be validated
issue_body:
Because we pop those fields before validating them with the collection schema.

We can either:
* prevent those fields to be mentioned as `required` when defining the collection schema
* or not pop them if they are present in the schema before validating

issue_title: Bot sending wrong Quick-Chat messages
issue_body:
Since the [RLBot Framework version 0.0.28](https://github.com/RLBot/RLBot/pull/202) the quick-chat messages seems to have some weird offset leading to the bot sending the wrong messages.

Results with python bot and message visibility `CHAT_EVERYONE` using the QuickChatSelection [here](https://github.com/RLBot/RLBot/blob/master/src/main/flatbuffers/rlbot.fbs):

    expected Id in code -> actual message id

    0 -> 36
    1 -> 37
    10 -> 8
    20 -> 48
    21 -> 50
    33 -> 4
    34 -> 2
    55 -> 19
    57 -> 16

issue_title: Add support for detection evaluation
issue_body: The CSV loader is assuming that the two files will be the same length, this is not the case for detection challenges. Annotations will still be made for each case, but the numbers may be different. Jonas might have something that does the hit detection already.

issue_title: Pinned transitive dependencies are not resolved
issue_body:
Consider the following scenario: my conda env already has package `A` at version `x`. I `conda install D`, which depends on `C`, which depends on `B`, which depends on `A` pinned at version `y > x`. There is no conflict of `A` version `y` with any other package.

`conda install D` does *not* upgrade `A`, leaving `D` in a broken state.

My concrete example involves packages on conda-forge (which I believe should not make a difference) and the packages in question are: `magics` dependens on `python-eccodes` depends on `jasper` depends on `jpeg 9.*`. The Azure notebooks root env provides `jpeg 8d`. `conda install -y -c conda-forge magics` does *not* upgrade `jpeg`.

issue_title: Allow choosing AWS S3 profiles for S3-based channels?
issue_body:
I was wondering if it was possible to allow specifying S3 profile names?

It's not uncommon to have multiple AWS credentials stored, so currently you have to do some environment variable munging like

```sh
$ AWS_DEFAULT_PROFILE_NAME=my_profile_1 conda some stuff
```

Even worse, it's impossible to have two S3 channels at once that require different credentials:

```yaml
channels:
    - s3://channel-1
    - s3://channel-2
```

If the channels are just strings, maybe a hack like this would work?

```yaml

issue_title: Detect if Java is not installed, or the installed version is not supported
issue_body:
Command execution:
```Python
try:
	subprocess.check_call(["java", "-cp", _classpath(), "org.jpmml.sklearn.Main"])
except CalledProcessError:
	raise RuntimeError("The JPMML-SkLearn conversion application has failed.")
```

The message of the `RuntimeError` suggests that the `CalledProcessError` was thrown because the Java executable errored out (ie. exited with a non-zero exit code). However, a `CalledProcessError` can also be thrown if the Java executable (eg. `java.exe`) was not found on the system path, or if it was a wrong version (eg. Java 1.6, which is too old, or Java 1.9, which is too new).

The SkLearn2PMML package should try to detect the availability of the Java executable, and if present, determine its version to assist end users in their troubleshooting efforts.

issue_title: Session.close() fails when using transactions in some cases
issue_body:
I have this test code. Since the docs stress that the session should be closed whatever happens, I have an session.close() in the try/except/finally construct. 

There seems to be a difference between an exception in cypher (error case 1) and a regular exception (see error case 2).  In the first case I get an error in the line session.close(): "neo4j.v1.exceptions.CypherError: rollback cannot be done when there is no open transaction in the session.” In the second case (of course commenting out case 1): no error and the tx is rolled back correctly.

```
from neo4j.v1 import GraphDatabase, basic_auth, TRUST_ON_FIRST_USE

driver = GraphDatabase.driver("bolt://localhost:7000",
                              auth=basic_auth("neo4j", "test"),
                              encrypted=False,
                              trust=TRUST_ON_FIRST_USE)

session = driver.session()
tx = session.begin_transaction()

try:
    result = tx.run("CREATE (n:Person{lastname:'test', firstname:'T'})")
    # Error case 1: with the following 2 lines uncommentend one gets the problem
    result = tx.run("XXMATCH (n:Person)-[r]->(m) RETURN n, r, m")
    result = list(result)
    # Error case 2: with the following line uncommented instead of the previous two one does not get the problem
    # raise Exception('Testing exception')

    tx.commit()
except Exception as e:
    print('*** ',e)
    tx.rollback()
finally:
    session.close()
```

issue_title: Set negative values of AOI loss to 0 rather than np.nan
issue_body:
The current AOI loss models set negative values to np.nan, rather than zero.  When np.nans are propagated to the effective irradiance model, the beam irradiance (and the effective irradiance) becomes np.nan rather than 0.  As a consequence, pvlib cannot compute effective irradiance when AOI>90, i.e., when the sun is behind the array plane.  In this circumstance there's still diffuse irradiance on the array plane.

I think it would be better to implement the AOI loss models as

f(AOI) = model value if AOI<90
f(AOI) = 0 otherwise.

issue_body:
In gitlab by @sylvaingaudan on May 17, 2017, 09:39

Add automaticaly CollectionReferences if
- configuration has automaticCollectionReferencement.enable=true
- index has an id, a timestamp date, a centroid and a geometry

To detect if the index has those fields, the arlas configuration provides prefered names for those fields:
```
automaticCollectionReferencement.preferredIdFieldName='id,identifier'
automaticCollectionReferencement.preferredTimestampFieldName='timestamp'

automaticCollectionReferencement.preferredGeometryFieldName='geo,geometry'

This automatic referencement is launched as a dropwizard admin task:

Possibly, it could also be done regularily if 
```
automaticCollectionReferencement.schedule 
```
is configured (see dropwizard LifecycleEnvironment#scheduledExecutorService)

issue_title: '$.attr' returns undefined for boolean inputs even if they are in attribute list
issue_body:
This is pretty minor, but it's an actual bug in production for us, and I couldn't find any issues that seemed to cover this.

##### Overview

jQuery migrate causes `$.attr` to return undefined for boolean attributes if the requested boolean's property is false, even if the attribute is not. I am experiencing this problem because Firefox is incorrectly giving a checkbox input a property of `checked: false` on a live site. This is causing jQuery migrate to make `$el.attr('checked')` return `false`, although it should be returning `"checked"` because "checked=checked" is still in the attributes list.

##### Versions

This is using jQuery version 1.11.2 and jQuery migrate 1.2.1, although I believe the versions are irrelevant.

##### Simple Reproduction

Make a checkbox element that is checked, then set it's checked property to false. Then try to get its checked attribute and it will return undefined.
```
var $input = $('<input id="shippingIsBilling" name="shippingIsBilling" checked="checked" class="js-shippingIsBilling" type="checkbox">').appendTo('body')
$input.get(0).checked = false;
$input.get(0).outerHTML
// "<input id="shippingIsBilling" name="shippingIsBilling" checked="checked" class="js-shippingIsBilling" type="checkbox">"
$input.attr('checked')
// undefined
$input.prop('checked')
// false
```

##### Why it's a bug

jQuery without the migrate plugin returns "checked" for `$input.attr('checked')`. However, the logic for the migrate plugin forces boolean attributes to agree with `$.prop` in order to return true - https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/master/src/attributes.js#L46

##### Proposed Solution

I think that if an item is in the attributes list, it should probably return that from a `$.attr` call, instead of short-circuiting to a ( in this case, incorrect ) call to `prop`.

##### Live Reproduction

I'm only actually experiencing this problem in Firefox ( I'm on version 40.0.3, but also experienced it in 40.0.2 ). It works as expected in Chrome 44.0.2403.157.

This bug should be able to be recreated on https://www.smartdestinations.com/checkout/sdi-checkout.ep . You'll need to [add an item to your cart](http://www.smartdestinations.com/pass.ep?filters=_d_Bos_Prod_Go) first. Then, go to checkout but don't do anything. You should see 3 delivery options. Refresh the page. If all went right ( wrong? ), you should now see 5 delivery options, and can explore the bug.

You can now run the following snippets in the console:

```
$(".js-shippingIsBilling").get(0).outerHTML
// "<input id="shippingIsBilling" name="shippingIsBilling" checked="checked" class="js-shippingIsBilling" type="checkbox">"
$(".js-shippingIsBilling").attr('checked')
// undefined
$(".js-shippingIsBilling").prop('checked')
// false
```

jQuery without the migrate plugin returns "checked" for `$(".js-shippingIsBilling").attr('checked')`. I tested locally that this is true - there's not a public version of the page without the migrate plugin.

I believe this is some kind of bug in Firefox caching input values, although the input is never being set to false, so that doesn't REALLY make sense.

I think Firefox should stop doing whatever it's doing that's messing this up, and if it did, I wouldn't care about this "bug" being fixed in jQuery migrate. I'm not sure how to describe it any more clearly than this report, so I'll probably just link this in bugzilla, if it will let me.

issue_title: Support for Fetch environments?
issue_body:
It seems like baselines is not directly implemented to deal with `Box()` type action spaces.  This same exact code works for the `CartPole` environment.  It fails on `FetchReach-v1`.  Here is the code:

```
import gym
from baselines import deepq


def callback(lcl, _glb):
    # stop training if reward exceeds 199
    is_solved = lcl['t'] > 100 and sum(lcl['episode_rewards'][-101:-1]) / 100 >= 199
    return is_solved


def main():
    env = gym.make("FetchReach-v1")
    model = deepq.models.mlp([64])
    act = deepq.learn(
        env,
        q_func=model,
        lr=1e-3,
        max_timesteps=100000,
        buffer_size=50000,
        exploration_fraction=0.1,
        exploration_final_eps=0.02,
        print_freq=10,
        callback=callback
    )
    print("Saving model to Fetch_model.pkl")
    act.save("Fetch_model.pkl")


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

When I try to use the same `deepq` algorithm trained on `cartpole`, with a discrete action space, on `FetchReach-V1`, I get the following:
```
File "train_FetchReach.py", line 31, in <module>
    main()
  File "train_FetchReach.py", line 24, in main
    callback=callback
  File "/home/jeremy/.local/share/virtualenvs/cgw-i4TbRcn4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/baselines/deepq/simple.py", line 180, in learn
    num_actions=env.action_space.n,
AttributeError: 'Box' object has no attribute 'n'
```

I tried adding

`env.action_space.n = len(env.action_space.sample())`

but that just lead to more errors:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "train_FetchReach.py", line 32, in <module>
    main()
  File "train_FetchReach.py", line 25, in main
    callback=callback
  File "/home/jeremy/.local/share/virtualenvs/cgw-i4TbRcn4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/baselines/deepq/simple.py", line 184, in learn
    param_noise=param_noise
  File "/home/jeremy/.local/share/virtualenvs/cgw-i4TbRcn4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/baselines/deepq/build_graph.py", line 376, in build_train
    act_f = build_act(make_obs_ph, q_func, num_actions, scope=scope, reuse=reuse)
  File "/home/jeremy/.local/share/virtualenvs/cgw-i4TbRcn4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/baselines/deepq/build_graph.py", line 177, in build_act
    observations_ph = make_obs_ph("observation")
  File "/home/jeremy/.local/share/virtualenvs/cgw-i4TbRcn4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/baselines/deepq/simple.py", line 175, in make_obs_ph
    return BatchInput(observation_space_shape, name=name)

    super().__init__(tf.placeholder(dtype, [None] + list(shape), name=name))
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable

issue_title: Allow specifying `deleteBeforeReplace` on a specific resource
issue_body:
The engine now supports delete-before-replace as an option, but the only way to opt-in is at the resource provider level.

It would be nice if users could explicitly opt-in to delete-before-replace on specific resource objects using a `ResourceOptions` flag, such as:

```typescript
new aws.rds.Instance("db", {}, {deleteBeforeReplace: true});
```

issue_title: Inheritance and py::cast
py::cast<T> where T is a subclass of py::object is unchecked
Stripe Timeout on App Engine Is Too Short When Being Called from TaskQueue
issue_body:
Currently, on Google App Engine, the stripe client times out if a request takes more than 55 seconds.  The reason for this (see code below), is because Google App Engine has a 60 second timeout for normal requests.  However, App Engine backends and TaskQueues are not subject to this deadline.

It is a common usage scenario to do a lot of heavy lifting for billing on a backend (e.g., running reports, generating invoices, etc.).  Often the 55 second timeout is too short and the request times out.  

Please increase this timeout, or better yet, detect when running in a TaskQueue and increase it only in that situation.  Thank you.

stripe.__init__.py:427
```
    # GAE requests time out after 60 seconds, so make sure we leave
    # some time for the application to handle a slow Stripe
    args['deadline'] = 55
```

issue_title: Difference of valid variable names between Matlab and Python
issue_body: In Matlab, the variable names beginning with underline(_) are not the valid variable. However, it is valid in Python. So it might cause some problems. Although it is a tiny problem, I think we should specify that in the documentation.

issue_title: Remote task file issue with localhost
issue_body:
This is related to #822 . Let me try to describe it clearly:

My SoS command is:

```
sos run ... -r midway2
```

which first connects to the remote host `midway2` then submit jobs from there. On that remote host I set `localhost` to be the batch job template, which is also called `midway2` in my `hosts.yml` -- you see as resolution to #822  I updated the documentation with the tip that if a remote host  is a batch system one should set `localhost` to the batch system. 

However I get this error message:

```

issue_title: sys.path not being correctly set up
issue_body:
Given the `foo/bar.py` with `import subdir` and an empty `foo/subdir/__init__.py`, running `python foo/bar.py` exits with no exceptions printed and with an exit code of zero, however `python -m vmprof foo/bar.py` throws ImportError:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/pypy/lib-python/2.7/runpy.py", line 162, in _run_module_as_main
    "__main__", fname, loader, pkg_name)
  File "/opt/pypy/lib-python/2.7/runpy.py", line 72, in _run_code
    exec code in run_globals
  File "/mnt/home-extra/gsnedders/Documents/other-projects/csswg-testsuite-pypy-env/site-packages/vmprof/__main__.py", line 69, in <module>

  File "/opt/pypy/lib-python/2.7/runpy.py", line 240, in run_path

issue_title: zmq.asyncio is broken (examples inside)
issue_body:
This code works as expected (using normal asyncio module):
```python
import asyncio
queue = asyncio.Queue()

async def pushing():
    while True:
        await queue.put("hello")
        await asyncio.sleep(1)

async def pulling():
    while True:
        greeting = await queue.get()
        print(greeting)

def asyncio_loop():
    import asyncio
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    tasks = [
        pushing(),
        pulling()
    ]
    loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.wait(tasks))
    loop.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio_loop()
```

Whereas the code using zmq.asyncio hangs (it's not resuming the functions):

```python
import asyncio
queue = asyncio.Queue()

async def pushing():
    while True:
        await queue.put("hello")
        await asyncio.sleep(1)

async def pulling():
    while True:
        greeting = await queue.get()
        print(greeting)

def zmq_async_loop():
    import zmq.asyncio
    loop = zmq.asyncio.install()
    tasks = [
        pushing(),
        pulling()
    ]
    loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.wait(tasks))
    loop.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    zmq_async_loop()
```

issue_title: Two tweaks from "layercake"
issue_body:
gjhigg...@gmail.com, 2010-08-06T03:31:41.000Z 
Two small changes to rdflib.term by Chimezie made to his 2.4.X "layercake-python" setup:

http://groups.google.com/group/fuxi-discussion/msg/d9f3be240efb81ab 

They don't appear to have been propagated to the rdflib main branch, so I'll simply post the diff for immediacy of inspection (with an attached diff for convenience) :

Index: /opt/rdflib-svn/rdflib/term.py
============================================
--- /opt/rdflib-svn/rdflib/term.py	(revision 1890)
+++ /opt/rdflib-svn/rdflib/term.py	(working copy)
@@ -129,6 +129,8 @@
     def __eq__(self, other):
         if isinstance(other, URIRef):
             return unicode(self)==unicode(other)
+        elif isinstance(other,basestring):
+           return unicode(self)==other
         else:
             return False
 
@@ -719,7 +721,10 @@
     return strptime(v, "%H:%M:%S")
 
 def _strToDate(v) :
-    tstr = strptime(v, "%Y-%m-%d")
+    try:
+        tstr = strptime(v,"%Y-%m-%d")
+    except:
+        tstr = strptime(v,"%Y-%m-%dZ")
     return date(tstr.tm_year, tstr.tm_mon, tstr.tm_mday)
 
 def _strToDateTime(v) :

Comment 1 by gromgull
I do not think this patch is correct - applying it introduces several test failures. 
In particular, once applied rdflib.URIRef("urn:a") == rdflib.Literal("urn:a") and this should not be the case.


Comment 2 by gjhigg...@gmail.com
I posted the patch as a possible convenience for repos maintainers- it was just a diff that dropped out of my explorations of the details of the layercake/rdflib3 split.

I've also not helped matters by conflating two small changes, one to term.__eq__() and another to term._strToDate().

You're clearly correct that the patch to __eq__() should be discarded.

I suspect that the patch to _strToDate can also be safely discarded, it makes no difference to the test results whether the patch is applied or not.


Comment 3 by gromgull
I went through layercake changes and applied good stuff a while back - closing this.

issue_title: long output display issue
issue_body: This may very likely be a issue on matlab side, just wonder if there is a clever way to process it on matlab_kernel side.  whenever you have a large data set (e.g,a matrix that have several million row) as output and if you forget to use ";" to suppress output, it will try to copy all "millions" rows over and display it entirely. This simply freeze the whole front-end and render it useless.

issue_title: Add stanza rename detection to `compare_cfgs()`
issue_body:
Expand compare_cfgs() to detect stanza renames.  Lots of challenges with this such as how `DiffOp` currently doesn't allow for such output.  Could use `difflib.get_close_matches` with a high cutoff=0.9? 

Alternatively, detection could be based on common values.   (Could work well for savesearches, but would be very difficult for other less uniquely identifiable content like props.)

This would require significant work and likely would require rework of the output of the Diff objects (which currently have no way to express renames.)  Perhaps rename detection could be handled outside of (or before) the actual diff output?

issue_title: Allow Certbot to run in parallel
issue_body:
In 0.14.0 we added simple lockfiles to prevent concurrently running Certbot instances from corrupting your files. As you can see in https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/4449#issuecomment-299802507, some people had been running Certbot concurrently though. While doing this was unsafe (and undocumented), it'd be nice if things like certificate renewal could be run in parallel to speed up things for large integrations.

Doing this would be a significant increase in complexity to Certbot's locking mechanism though.

issue_title: tos as url (or true) in renewal/:hostname.conf
issue_body:
I see that `:conf/renewal/:hostname.conf`has a field `tos = True` and I couldn't find anything out about it [in the docs](http://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/search.html?q=renewal&check_keywords=yes&area=default), but I'd like to suggest that it allow either a string url or True.

The **purpose of the string url** is that if the tos changes and the user has decided (in some user interface) that they would not like certificates to renew until they have had the **opportunity to view the revised terms** to which they will be **legally bound** that the renewal should fail.

I know this seems unlikely, but there are some people who take this stuff very seriously and others [who don't](http://www.foxnews.com/tech/2010/04/15/online-shoppers-unknowingly-sold-souls.html).

As of the current date I think it's generally agreed upon that Mozilla and the EFF have goals and values that align with their customers. And of course they always will be, forever (maybe).

But other companies may step up to the plate that do not have their customers' best interests in mind - those who use free ssl as a marketing tactic and later abuse their customers privacy or other rights. It's uncomfortable to unwittingly sell your immortal soul, especially *automatically*.

* http://www.foxnews.com/tech/2010/04/15/online-shoppers-unknowingly-sold-souls.html

issue_title: `ManyToManyField`s on `Page` subclasses have the wrong through table name created during migrations
issue_body:
Using `Django==1.7.10` or 'Django==1.8.4.` there appears to to be an issue where the through table for a `ManyToManyField` gets made with an incorrect name.

Using the following model structure in a base project:

    from django.db import models
    from fluent_pages.models import Page


    class BasicPage(Page):
        product_category = models.ManyToManyField(
            'sites.Site'
        )

        class Meta:
            verbose_name = 'Basic page'
            verbose_name_plural = 'Basic pages'

I ran `./manage.py makemigrations <app_name>` which generated a migration which looks sensible:

    # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
    from __future__ import unicode_literals

    from django.db import models, migrations


    class Migration(migrations.Migration):

        dependencies = [
            ('fluent_pages', '0002_auto_20150901_0528'),
            ('sites', '0001_initial'),
        ]

        operations = [
            migrations.CreateModel(
                name='BasicPage',
                fields=[
                    ('urlnode_ptr', models.OneToOneField(parent_link=True, auto_created=True, primary_key=True, serialize=False, to='fluent_pages.UrlNode')),
                    ('product_category', models.ManyToManyField(to='sites.Site')),
                ],
                options={
                    'db_table': 'pagetype_fluent_test_basicpage',
                    'verbose_name': 'Basic page',
                    'verbose_name_plural': 'Basic pages',
                },
                bases=('fluent_pages.page',),
            ),
        ]

So all is migrated and I go into the admin and try to save some many to many data:

    OperationalError at /admin/fluent_pages/page/add/
    no such table: fluent_test_basicpage_product_category

After fishing around it appears that during the Django life cycle it does not have that name except when in the migration process the `CreateModel.database_forwards` function gets the app state at which point the `URLNodeMetaClass.__new__` table changes have been applied to it which can be seen by putting a debug set trace at after the model retrieval in `CreateModel.database_forwards` and printing `model._meta.get_field('product_category').rel.through._meta.db_table`. If you then run in shell mode (`./manage.py shell`) and import your model and do the same you will notice a different table name.

The output for each of these are:
Migration --> u'pagetype_fluent_test_basicpage_product_category'
Shell --> u'fluent_test_basicpage_product_category'

I am happy to help look into this issue but if anyone has an ideas or knowledge that would be awesome!

As a work around I guess you could manually create the through table manually and set the db table on it? I have not tested this though.

issue_title: Inconsistent compatibility versioning + outdated README
issue_body:
The README needs to be updated, because it currently states the following version compatibility:

```
# Elasticsearch 1.0
elasticsearch>=1.0.0,<2.0.0
```

But that is not the case.  I recently installed 1.7.0 and it broke our calls to `put_mapping` because `ignore_conflicts` was removed as a parameter for 2.0 compatibility in commit 36d8d4b545938fd37988b72ccb8e19b58fe62882.

Perhaps the ES2.0 compatible versions of elasticsearch-py should start with v2.0.0, and leave <2.0.0 for ES1.0?  Ideally it'd look something like this:

```
# Elasticsearch 2.0
elasticsearch>=2.0.0,<3.0.0

# Elasticsearch 1.0
elasticsearch>=1.0.0,<2.0.0
```

issue_title: Bug switching backend between inline and notebook
issue_body:
I routinely switch back and forth between `notebook` and `inline` backends. Occasionally the `notebook` backend does not show the figure but an empty space, and a static image is shown in the output area below.

Usually when this happens I get messages like this in the terminal:

```
[IPKernelApp] ERROR | No such comm: c1fa5e1e9de8413692476a2c8fdf34bb
```

It seems to me that the probability of seeing this bug increases with "heavy" plots. I tried to create a small notebook showing the problem:

https://gist.github.com/tritemio/a4554323fe0faca85c57

However most of the times the previous notebook only triggers the `No such comm` message but the `notebook` figure still shows correctly.

I have a much more complex code that triggers the but much more often though.

attn: @tacaswell

issue_title: Removing the play button introduced in 5.6.0?
issue_body:
I understand the rationale of trying this feature out, but I worry that it becomes a feature that if we keep this change now we will have a harder time deprecating it in the future. We don't have enough information about it to keep it as the new default, and in a day of usage, I find it to be visual noise and frustrating. 

One particular problem with the current implementation is target area for executing a cell in place fills the entire vertical column, but the icon is only at the top.

Alternatives paths forward could be to provide this as an option, or to patch the release taking this feature out and put it back in in `master` to let it bake for longer. 

I feel that a UI change like this is significant and I think that the cost to current users who do not need it is too great for the benefit it provides to those who want such a feature.

issue_title: Bad version sequence
issue_body:
Back in Oct 2016 you increased the version to 0.12.0 in 56c16b809c48f2c and now in Dec 2017 you have gone back to 0.11.2 in 10919f6a9fe4151

I'm not sure why the earlier commit doesn't show in the master/lesscpy/init.py history but v0.12.0 was previously visible in pypi, now going back to 0.11.2 breaks packaging systems that had previously installed 0.12.0. Due to 0.12.0 no longer existing it leaves the current version as less than existing installed version.

issue_title: Allow passing memoryview/buffers to send
issue_body:
As nanomsg doesn't provide multipart messages it's convenient to create a BytesIO and dump a sequence of pickles into it. That way the "parts" of the message can be recovered at the other side by means of an equivalent sequence of pickle loads.

But the current implementation of Socket.send in nnpy requires the memoryview associated with the BytesIO to be transformed to bytes first:

```
TypeError: initializer for ctype 'char[19]' must be a bytes or list or tuple, not memoryview
```

This is not efficient, as it implies an extra copy of the message. But it seems the cffi guys implemented a conversion from buffer to char array:

https://bitbucket.org/cffi/cffi/issues/65/support-memoryview-to-char-conversion
https://bitbucket.org/cffi/cffi/issues/47/creating-a-cdata-from-a-buffer

I think it's important to support this in nnpy, as nanomsg is intended to be fast and require as little copy as possible.

issue_title: Kernels not connecting when missing `interact.json` file
issue_body:
While trying to get the install up on `master`, my kernel wasn't actually connecting. Popping up the dev tools showed that `~/Library/Jupyter/interact.json` was missing. After creating the file with an empty object in it, the kernel connected just fine.

I would suggest that the file be automatically created if it doesn't exist, but I don't know the implications to the rest of the system.

cc @rgbkrk and @jdetle who helped diagnose the issue on slack.

issue_title: compute() function not implemented in CLAClassifierRegion.py
issue_body:
The `compute` [1] function is not implemented. Consequently - after creating a Network instance with a CLAClassifierRegion - `network.run()` won't feed data to the CLAClassifier correctly, and we have to use the `customComputeFunction` [2] in an outer loop [3].

[1] https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/regions/CLAClassifierRegion.py#L222-L232
[2] https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/regions/CLAClassifierRegion.py#L235-L263
[3] https://github.com/numenta/nupic.research/blob/ab3bc24e1523d604723bb872ff2e6ac2b0dc8f53/classification/classify_active_cells.py#L157-L163

issue_title: Show a banner for inter-capture navigation and interaction w/ ipwb when viewing a memento

Related to #26

Most archival replay systems have a banner splayed across the top of the capture to allow inter-capture navigation. ipwb might benefit from this by also providing additional functionality in this banner to allow interaction with the system from the web interface without going back to the root page. Mock-ups / bulleted list of features to come.

issue_body:
This year we're using SendableChooser again to select an autonomous mode from the SmartDashboard.  This year in our shop environment (wifi) it is failing to use the selected auto mode.  Oddly, this worked fine at competition last week when connected to the field.  

Looking at the code, it appears that the following call in sendablechooser.py, line 90 in getSelected() is not working as expected:

`table = self.getTable()`

The "getTable" call always returns None, which causes it to use the default mode, regardless of the selection.  If no default mode/object is defined it will throw an exception trying to look up "None" in the map.

This is using pyfrc 2017.1.3, robotpy 2017.1.2, on Windows 10.
